tool_args.label: New York City Transit Key Performance Metrics - September 2024
tool_args.raw_text: NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS September 2024

payload.title: NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.description: Key performance metrics document prepared for the September 2024 meeting of the New York City Transit & Bus Committee
payload.prepared_for: New York City Transit & Bus Committee

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Demetrius Crichlow, Interim President New York City Transit
tool_args.raw_text: Demetrius Crichlow Interim President New York City Transit

payload.title: Interim President New York City Transit

evidence_refs.source_quote: Demetrius Crichlow Interim President New York City Transit

tool_args.label: Hugo Zamora
tool_args.raw_text: Hugo Zamora, our project lead on the Crosstown CBTC installation project

payload.description: Project lead on the Crosstown CBTC installation project

evidence_refs.source_quote: Hugo Zamora, our project lead on the Crosstown CBTC installation project

tool_args.label: Michael J. Quill Bus Depot
tool_args.raw_text: tour of the Michael J. Quill Bus Depot to discuss efforts and challenges in their transition to a zero-emissions bus fleet by 2040. With 15 electric buses already at this depot

payload.description: Bus depot with 15 electric buses, part of transition to zero-emissions bus fleet

tool_args.label: Bus Fare Enforcement Initiative
tool_args.raw_text: In response, we launched an expanded fare enforcement initiative on August 29, deploying the NYPD and NYCT's EAGLE team to ticket and remove fare evaders. These efforts, reaching over 40 locations across all five boroughs

payload.description: NYCT expanded fare enforcement initiative deploying NYPD and EAGLE team to ticket and remove fare evaders, reaching over 40 locations across all five boroughs

tool_args.label: Modernizing the G Train - Crosstown CBTC Installation
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development successfully completed this summer's major planned work to begin modernizing the signal system on the Crosstown Line, which is serviced by the G train. ... The next stages of this project which will continue until Q3 2027. Upon completion, the G will be the third subway line in the NYCT system to run entirely on CBTC

payload.status: service restored September 3, 2024; continues until Q3 2027
payload.description: Installing Communications Based Train Control (CBTC) on the Crosstown Line (G train). Completed summer 2024 major work installing signal cables and equipment across entire line, reconstructed Court Square interlocking, and replaced approximately 30 miles of track. More than 100 miles of signal cable and more than 300 transponders installed.

tool_args.label: ACE Expansion - 10 new bus routes
tool_args.raw_text: On September 16, we announced a new 60-day warning period for the ACE program on 10 new bus routes. This is in addition to the 14 routes that already have ACE activated. ... On September 30, we will start the 60-day warning period on another 10 bus routes.

payload.status: 60-day warning period started September 16, 2024 on 10 new bus routes; additional 10 routes starting September 30, 2024
payload.description: Expansion of Automated Camera Enforcement (ACE) adding 10 new bus routes on September 16, 2024 and another 10 on September 30, 2024. Total reaching 34 routes and over 1,000 ACE-equipped buses across all five boroughs.

tool_args.label: Culver Line CBTC
tool_args.raw_text: we were happy to bring normal service back to the G train and finish the installation of CBTC on the Culver line. ... We're also thrilled that CBTC is coming to the Culver line. These new signals will make travel throughout southern Brooklyn faster and more efficient.

payload.description: Completion of CBTC installation on the Culver line. New signals will make travel through southern Brooklyn faster and more efficient.

tool_args.label: Student OMNY Card Program
tool_args.raw_text: Back to school has brought with it our new Student OMNY program. ... 1.5 million Student OMNY cards distributed ... These cards, which are now active 365 days a year and 7 days a week

payload.description: New Student OMNY program for NYC public school students. Cards active 365 days a year, 7 days a week. 1.5 million Student OMNY cards distributed.

tool_args.label: MTA 2025-2029 Capital Program
tool_args.raw_text: The proposed plan outlines $68.4 billion worth of investments over the next five years to make sure New Yorkers continue to have access to reliable, accessible, and sustainable transit. ... include ordering 1,500 new railcars, installing modern CBTC signals on at least 75 miles of the subway system, modernizing the shops at Livonia and 240 St, rebuilding or repairing 66 substations, renewing 24 miles of elevated structures and starting the construction of the Interborough Express (IBX)

payload.description: Proposed plan outlines $68.4 billion worth of investments over five years. Includes ordering 1,500 new railcars, installing modern CBTC signals on at least 75 miles of subway system, modernizing shops at Livonia and 240 St, rebuilding or repairing 66 substations, renewing 24 miles of elevated structures, and starting construction of the Interborough Express (IBX).

tool_args.label: Reduced-Fare OMNY Transition
tool_args.raw_text: We are on pace to mail all of our Reduced-Fare customers a new OMNY card by the end of the year. Think about this: there are approximately 1.5 million Reduced-Fare customers.

payload.description: Mailing all Reduced-Fare customers a new OMNY card by end of 2024. Approximately 1.5 million Reduced-Fare customers.

tool_args.label: G Train service restored
tool_args.raw_text: Service was successfully restored on the full G line on September 3, on time.

payload.description: Full G line service restored after nine weeks of partial shutdowns over summer for CBTC construction work

tool_args.label: ACE 60-day warning period on 10 new routes
tool_args.raw_text: On September 16, we announced a new 60-day warning period for the ACE program on 10 new bus routes.

payload.event_name: ACE 60-day warning period on 10 new bus routes
payload.description: New 60-day warning period for ACE program on 10 new bus routes, in addition to 14 routes already with ACE activated

tool_args.label: ACE 60-day warning period on additional 10 routes
tool_args.raw_text: On September 30, we will start the 60-day warning period on another 10 bus routes.

payload.event_name: ACE 60-day warning period on additional 10 bus routes
payload.description: Additional 10 bus routes start 60-day warning period for ACE

tool_args.label: Bus fare enforcement initiative launch
tool_args.raw_text: we launched an expanded fare enforcement initiative on August 29, deploying the NYPD and NYCT's EAGLE team to ticket and remove fare evaders.

payload.description: Launch of expanded fare enforcement initiative deploying NYPD and NYCT's EAGLE team to ticket and remove fare evaders

tool_args.label: New elevators opened at 14 St - 6 Av Station
tool_args.raw_text: in late August we opened three new elevators at 14 St - 6 Av Station. The L train at 6 Av is now accessible in both directions as well as the uptown FM platform.

payload.event_name: New elevators opened at 14 St - 6 Av Station
payload.description: Three new elevators opened at 14 St - 6 Av Station. L train at 6 Av now accessible in both directions as well as uptown FM platform.

tool_args.label: Demetrius Crichlow's 100th day as Interim President
tool_args.raw_text: September also marks my 100th day on the job as Interim President. ... On my 100th day, we opened new employee facilities at Chambers Street and Hoyt-Schermerhorn

payload.event_name: Demetrius Crichlow's 100th day as Interim President
payload.description: 100th day on the job as Interim President of New York City Transit. Opened new employee facilities at Chambers Street and Hoyt-Schermerhorn.

tool_args.label: Subway weekday On-Time Performance August 2024
tool_args.raw_text: For August 2024, weekday On-Time Performance (OTP) was 81.8%

tool_args.label: Subway Service Delivered August 2024
tool_args.raw_text: Service Delivered was 93.9%

tool_args.label: Subway Customer Journey Time Performance August 2024
tool_args.raw_text: the percentage of customers arriving at their destinations within five minutes of schedule (CJTP) was 84.7%

tool_args.label: Bus Service Delivered August 2024
tool_args.raw_text: Service Delivered (SD) stood at 94%

tool_args.label: Bus average speed August 2024
tool_args.raw_text: Bus speeds averaged 8.2 mph

tool_args.label: Bus Customer Journey Time Performance August 2024
tool_args.raw_text: Customer Journey Time Performance (CJTP) by 0.3% to 73.2% in August

tool_args.label: Bus fare evasion statement
tool_args.raw_text: In 2020, about 21% of bus riders didn't pay their fare; now, that number is roughly 50%.

payload.claim_text: In 2020, about 21% of bus riders didn't pay their fare; now, that number is roughly 50%.
payload.subject: bus fare evasion

tool_args.label: Paid weekday subway ridership July 2024
tool_args.raw_text: Paid weekday subway ridership was 3.44 million ... in July

tool_args.label: Paid weekday subway ridership August 2024
tool_args.raw_text: Paid weekday subway ridership was 3.44 million and 3.35 million respectively in July and August

tool_args.label: G line daily ridership
tool_args.raw_text: roughly 160,000 daily riders between Brooklyn and Queens

payload.description: G line carries roughly 160,000 daily riders between Brooklyn and Queens

tool_args.raw_text: Paid weekday ridership was down 10% in both months from summer 2023, at 1.19 million and 1.15 million respectively

tool_args.raw_text: at 1.19 million and 1.15 million respectively

tool_args.label: Major Felony Crime July 2024 decrease
tool_args.raw_text: In July 2024, Major Felony Crime was down 14% month over month and down 14% versus July 2023. Crime also decreased by 21% when compared to the 2023 monthly average.

payload.claim_text: In July 2024, Major Felony Crime was down 14% month over month and down 14% versus July 2023. Crime also decreased by 21% when compared to the 2023 monthly average.

tool_args.label: Major Felony Crime August 2024 increase
tool_args.raw_text: For August 2024, Major Felony Crime was up 23% month over month and up 7% versus August 2023. But, Crime still decreased by 3% when compared to the 2023 monthly average.

payload.claim_text: For August 2024, Major Felony Crime was up 23% month over month and up 7% versus August 2023. But, Crime still decreased by 3% when compared to the 2023 monthly average.
